# statsguider

[![pkgdown](https://img.shields.io/badge/docs-pkgdown-315c86)](https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/)
[![R-CMD-check](https://github.com/dai540/statsguider/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/dai540/statsguider/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml)
[![License:
MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-green.svg)](https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/LICENSE)

`statsguider` is an R package for simple, guided statistical test
selection. It starts from a `data.frame`, asks for a small set of
study-design choices, then recommends an appropriate method or stops
when a simple test is not the right branch.

The package is designed for researchers who want a narrow and practical
workflow:

- choose the outcome column
- choose the group column
- declare whether the data are paired or repeated
- declare whether adjustment is needed
- run the recommended simple test when appropriate

`statsguider` is built around five main jobs:

- [`select_test()`](https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/reference/select_test.md)
  for the main one-shot interface
- [`guided_test()`](https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/reference/guided_test.md)
  for step-by-step branching
- [`recommend_test()`](https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/reference/recommend_test.md)
  for recommendation without execution
- [`run_test()`](https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/reference/run_test.md)
  for guarded execution of the selected method
- [`check_design()`](https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/reference/check_design.md)
  for stopping inappropriate simple analyses early

The package is intentionally narrow in version 1.0.0. It focuses on
single-outcome group comparison workflows and redirects users when the
data belong in regression, survival analysis, agreement analysis,
equivalence, or count-model branches.

## Installation

Install from GitHub:

``` r
install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("dai540/statsguider")
```

Or:

``` r
install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("dai540/statsguider")
```

Or install from a source tarball:

``` r
install.packages("path/to/statsguider_1.0.0.tar.gz", repos = NULL, type = "source")
```

Then load the package:

``` r
library(statsguider)
```

## Citation

If you use `statsguider`, cite the package as:

> Dai (2026). *statsguider: Guided Statistical Test Selection from a
> Data Frame*. R package. <https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/>

You can also retrieve the citation from R:

``` r
citation("statsguider")
```

## Fastest way to use it

The simplest entry point is
[`select_test()`](https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/reference/select_test.md).

``` r
dat <- subset(wet_example, visit == "week4")

select_test(
  data = dat,
  outcome = "biomarker",
  group = "group",
  outcome_type = "continuous",
  paired = "no",
  repeated = "no",
  run = "run"
)
```

## Core workflow

`statsguider` does three things.

- classifies the data into a simple branch
- recommends a method with a short explanation
- runs the method only when the branch is appropriate

In practice, the package is doing this:

1.  inspect the outcome type
2.  inspect whether groups are independent, paired, or repeated
3.  check whether a simple test is appropriate
4.  recommend a test such as Welch t-test, paired t-test, Fisher exact
    test, or Friedman test
5.  redirect when the question needs a model rather than a simple test

## Main functions

- [`select_test()`](https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/reference/select_test.md)
- [`guided_test()`](https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/reference/guided_test.md)
- [`recommend_test()`](https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/reference/recommend_test.md)
- [`run_test()`](https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/reference/run_test.md)
- [`check_design()`](https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/reference/check_design.md)

## Tutorial structure

The website is organized around:

- `Get Started`
  - package overview
  - branching logic
  - main functions
- `Articles`
  - English scenario tutorials
  - Japanese scenario tutorials
  - English and Japanese branching guides

Every scenario tutorial creates a small data table and applies
[`select_test()`](https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/reference/select_test.md)
or
[`run_test()`](https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/reference/run_test.md)
to it.

# Package index

## Main functions

- [`select_test()`](https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/reference/select_test.md)
  : Select and Optionally Run a Statistical Test
- [`guided_test()`](https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/reference/guided_test.md)
  : Guide a User Through Simple Test Selection
- [`recommend_test()`](https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/reference/recommend_test.md)
  : Recommend a Statistical Test from Simple Data Properties
- [`run_test()`](https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/reference/run_test.md)
  : Run a Recommended Statistical Test
- [`check_design()`](https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/reference/check_design.md)
  : Check Whether a Design Fits the Simple Branching Workflow

## Example data

- [`wet_example`](https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/reference/wet_example.md)
  : Example Wet-Lab Style Dataset
